metadata.name: Religious Service Attendance
metadata.description: Regular attendance at religious services is associated with substantially reduced mortality through social support, health-promoting behaviors, psychological resilience, and sense of meaning and purpose.

sources.citation: Li S, Stampfer MJ, Williams DR, VanderWeele TJ. Association of Religious Service Attendance With Mortality Among Women. JAMA Internal Medicine. 2016;176(6):777-785.
Chida Y, Steptoe A, Powell LH. Religiosity/Spirituality and Mortality: A Meta-analysis. Psychotherapy and Psychosomatics. 2009;78:81-90.

metadata.mechanismOfAction: Social support networks, health-promoting behaviors (less smoking/drinking), stress buffering, sense of meaning and purpose, meditation/prayer effects

applicability.notes: Based on Nurses' Health Study (N=74,534) with 16-year follow-up. Effects observed across denominations.

points.citation: Multiple times per week reduces mortality by 27%
